subject: UX & Research
title: Continuous Discovery Habits
description: A fast breakdown of Continuous Discovery Habits for builders.

hook.text: Teresa Torres says: talk to users every week. Not once a quarter. Not before a redesign. Every single week.

buildup.text: Continuous discovery replaces big research projects with small, regular touchpoints. Weekly learning.

discovery.text: Torres's framework: recruit from your product, automate scheduling, interview 30 minutes, synthesize same day.

twist.text: Teams that research quarterly bet big on stale data. Teams that research weekly make small bets on fresh data.

climax.text: Continuous discovery changes culture. When everyone talks to users weekly, empathy becomes instinct.

punchline.text: Talk to users every week. Continuous learning beats big research.

quiz.question: What is the core principle of Teresa Torres's continuous discovery?
options: Talk to users every week with small, regular touchpoints instead of big quarterly research projects
Run a large usability study once per year
Only research when a major redesign is planned
